# Introduction

Welcome to the Docusaurus sample documentation. This site demonstrates
how a docs-site deliverable looks when the probe is happy: a config
file at the project root, a content directory the config points at,
and at least one meaningful page serving as the homepage.

## What you'll find here

Tutorials, reference material, and an API guide live under this
docs tree. Docusaurus uses file-system routing — a file at
`docs/intro.md` serves at `/docs/intro`, `docs/guides/setup.md`
serves at `/docs/guides/setup`, and so on.

## Conventions

- Pages use MDX so React components can embed inline
- Frontmatter controls sidebar position, slug, and title
- Links between pages use relative paths that Docusaurus resolves
